title: cornus hongkongensis subsp. ferruginea
intro: Cornus hongkongensis subsp. ferruginea, also known as the Hong Kong dogwood or rusty dogwood, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cornaceae. It is primarily used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory problems, and supporting cardiovascular health. In herbal medicine, Cornus hongkongensis subsp. ferruginea is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is often used to treat conditions such as bronchitis and pneumonia.
